entities.title: Lord of Spirits (podcast)
entities.summary: Introduces the podcast 'Lord of Spirits,' explaining its focus on spiritual reality, Orthodox Christian traditions, and practical engagement with angels and demons. Outlines the hosts' backgrounds and goals for the show.

entities.title: materialism in Christian circles
entities.summary: Discusses how modern Christians often live as though the physical world is all that exists, presenting this as a problem for addressing spiritual issues.

entities.title: Enochic literature
entities.summary: Explains the origins of the title 'Lord of Spirits' in Enochic literature.

entities.title: gods (Bible)
entities.summary: Discusses the use of the term 'god' in various contexts within the Bible, challenging monotheistic interpretations by examining examples from Psalms and Exodus.

entities.title: Mount Herman
entities.summary: Explores Mount Herman's significance for divine events, discussing the concept of polytheism versus monotheism and ancient religious practices.

entities.title: guardian angels in Orthodox Christianity
entities.summary: Discusses the role of guardian angels in Orthodox Christianity and provides practical advice on how to deepen one's relationship with their guardian angel through prayer.

entities.title: Fall of Lucifer
entities.summary: Discusses ancient myths and their parallels with biblical narratives, focusing on the concept of divine succession and Lucifer’s fall.

entities.title: Multiple Apostasies Among Angels
entities.summary: Explores angelic rebellions, contrasting human sin with multiple apostasies among angels and referencing pagan myths for illustration.

entities.title: Creation of Giants and Nephilim
entities.summary: Discusses demonic origins, focusing on the creation of giants and nephilim through angelic-human unions.

entities.title: Fall of Angelic Beings in Tower of Babel Narrative
entities.summary: Explores the fall of angelic beings as depicted in the Tower of Babel narrative, contrasting it with Paul’s interpretation and Plato’s account.

entities.title: Satan vs. The Devil
entities.summary: Discusses the distinction between Satan and the devil, exploring their roles in Jewish tradition and Christian scripture, particularly focusing on the fall of Satan after Christ's ministry as described in Luke and Revelation.

entities.title: Nephilim
entities.summary: The biblical term used to describe giants in Genesis 6:4. The block discusses its meaning and cultural significance.

entities.title: Amalekites
entities.summary: Ancient tribal group mentioned in the Old Testament, connected to demonic rituals and warfare against giants.

glossary.definition: The belief that the physical world is all that exists, often used to describe how modern Christians live.
The belief in multiple gods or deities, often used to describe ancient religious practices and beliefs.
The belief in one god or deity, often used to describe modern religious categorizations.
The process by which God's authority or rule is passed from one generation to another.
The act of angels rebelling against God, often depicted in biblical narratives and ancient myths.
The origins of demonic entities or powers, often linked to angelic-human unions in ancient narratives.

glossary.term: Fall in Tower of Babel Narrative
glossary.definition: The fall or rebellion of angelic beings as depicted in the Tower of Babel narrative.

glossary.term: Roles of Satan and the Devil
glossary.definition: The roles attributed to Satan and the devil in Jewish tradition and Christian scripture, particularly focusing on their fall after Christ's ministry.
Giants in Genesis 6:4, described as the offspring of divine beings and human women.
Ancient tribal group mentioned in the Old Testament, connected to demonic rituals and warfare against giants.
Ancient tribal group mentioned in the Old Testament, connected to demonic rituals and warfare against giants.
Ancient tribal group mentioned in the Old Testament, connected to demonic rituals and warfare against giants.
Attribute human form, qualities or actions to God.
Concept in OT of divine beings meeting and making decisions.
An angelic being who serves as a protector and guide for individuals.
A concept of divine beings or entities gathered together to make decisions or provide guidance.
The concept that some angels rebelled against God and were cast out of heaven.
A formal religious service or ritual in a Christian context, often centered on the resurrection of Christ.
The central focus or presence of God in all moments, especially within liturgical contexts.
Imagery found in Daniel and Revelation, symbolizing the ascent of God and the transfer of kingdom.
A day of participation in God's rule, following ascension imagery found in Daniel and Revelation.
Divine beings that serve as messengers or attendants to God and are believed to play various roles in the spiritual world.
A term used in the Bible, derived from Greek and Semitic languages.
A verb that means 'cover' in Semitic languages.
A Jewish holiday where sins are covered throughout the year.
An offering made by the high priest to cover his own sins.

glossary.term: Lot determination for goat for Yahweh
glossary.definition: A process used in determining which animal would be sacrificed as a sin offering.
The blood of the goat that was chosen to cover sins and purify holy items.
A discussion or analysis of how Protestant groups interpret certain religious texts, in this case, the Day of Atonement ritual.
An ancient Hebrew book of laws and rituals.

glossary.term: New Testament references to day of atonement
glossary.definition: References in the New Testament to the Day of Atonement ritual, which is a Jewish holiday.
The idea that God convenes with other divine beings or entities.

glossary.term: Angelic beings as sons of God
glossary.definition: Belief that angels are considered children or offspring of God.
A metaphorical representation of Mount Sinai as a place where divine revelations and commands were given to Moses.
The final or ultimate Day of Atonement, often associated with eschatology (the study of the end times).
Visual representations and symbolic elements used in the Day of Atonement ritual.

glossary.term: Atoning work of Christ mediated by church
glossary.definition: The role of the church in mediating or facilitating the atonement work of Jesus Christ.

glossary.term: Suffering servant image in Isaiah chapter 53
glossary.definition: A metaphorical description of Jesus as a suffering servant, often found in Isaiah's writings.
An explanation or analysis of the crucifixion event from the perspective of the Gospel of Matthew.

glossary.term: Purification and consecration of the temple
glossary.definition: The process of making a place holy through purification and consecration rites, often applied to the Temple in Jerusalem.
The idea that atonement involves or is associated with cleansing or purifying actions.
The belief that Jesus' human experience, including his suffering and death, can be seen as an act of atonement for humanity.
The process by which God became human through the incarnation, often associated with Christ's earthly life.
The idea of believers or followers participating in or experiencing aspects of Jesus' suffering as a form of spiritual growth and devotion.
a Greek word used to describe resurrection, different from the one used for Lazarus' resurrection
an icon depicting eanosis (resurrection)
